text: Risk management is an important process for any project or business. It begins with identifying risks, such as financial losses or technical failures. Once risks are known, mitigation strategies are developed to reduce their impact or likelihood. This might include training staff or improving safety measures. However, not all risks can be prevented, so contingency plans are prepared. These are backup actions to take if something goes wrong. Finally, the process involves monitoring risks regularly. This helps track changes and adjust plans as needed. Good risk management helps organisations stay prepared and respond effectively to unexpected problems.
